editor_settings.title: RTX 5090 vs RTX 4090 for AI: Why The New Flagship Actually Matters
editor_settings.content:
RTX 5090 vs RTX 4090 for AI: Why The New Flagship Actually Matters

The RTX 4090 is still powerful.

But the RTX 5090 changed the conversation for AI and compute-heavy work.

If you only game, this may not matter much. If you run local AI, rendering, security benchmarks, or heavy creator workflows, it matters a lot.

The Viral Benchmark Claim

A tweet from May 11, 2026 compared RTX 5090 against RTX 4090 in MD5 password hash benchmarking, claiming the RTX 5090 reached 220 GH/s and pulled far ahead in the test.

NVIDIA’s RTX 5090 cracks MD5 password hashes at 220 GH/s and was tested on 231 million unique leaked passwords collected by Kaspersky from the dark web.

>One card cracked 48% in under one minute
>60% in less than one hour
>77% in one year

The speed gain over the RTX 4090 is… pic.twitter.com/exTm4pQvnF

— Raunak Yadush (@raunak_yadush) May 11, 2026

Important: use this as a performance signal, not a tutorial.

The useful takeaway is not “go crack passwords.” The useful takeaway is that RTX 5090 can separate itself from RTX 4090 in compute-heavy workloads.

Why RTX 5090 Has The Advantage

NVIDIA lists the RTX 5090 with Blackwell architecture and 32GB of GDDR7 memory.

That is a serious jump for people who care about local AI, big models, rendering, and creator work.

The RTX 4090 has 24GB of GDDR6X, which is still excellent. But 32GB gives more breathing room when a task is memory hungry.

For Gaming, The Answer Is Less Dramatic

For gaming, the RTX 4090 is still ridiculous.

If you already own one, you do not need to panic just because the 5090 is faster. Most games will still feel excellent at 4K, especially with DLSS.

The upgrade only starts making sense if you want the absolute top end or your workload pays you back for the extra speed.

For AI, The 5090 Is Easier To Justify

AI work is different from gaming.

VRAM can decide whether a model fits at all. Faster compute can decide whether a job feels smooth or painfully slow.

If your work includes local AI, image generation, model testing, rendering, or heavy GPU compute, the RTX 5090 is not just a luxury flex. It can be a real tool.

Should You Still Buy RTX 4090?

Yes, if the price is right.

The RTX 4090 remains one of the strongest 24GB consumer GPUs you can buy. It is still excellent for 4K gaming and strong for creator work.

But in 2026, I would not buy one close to RTX 5090 pricing. That is the line.

Compare live top-end RTX pricing before locking yourself into an older flagship:

[product-card:asus-tuf-gaming-oc-geforce-rtx-5090-32gb-graphics-card-32gb-gddr7-pcie-50-21760-cuda-cores-8k-support-triple-fan-cooling]


Final Verdict

RTX 5090 wins the top-end AI and compute conversation.

RTX 4090 still makes sense when discounted, especially for 4K gaming and 24GB creator workloads.

The smarter buy depends on price. If the 4090 is clearly cheaper, it still has teeth. If the gap is small, the 5090 is the cleaner high-end choice.

Sources Checked

  • NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 official page
  • NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 official page
  • ASUS ROG Strix RTX 4090 specs
  • Gigabyte AORUS RTX 4090 Master product page
